mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, weak labor market conditions signal deteriorating consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during post-shock recovery phases reflect rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat in 70 percent of observations during identified recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings force markets to price in tighter monetary policy and lower real equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window despite rising inflation readings.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth surpasses 2.0 percent, strong aggregate demand supports corporate revenue projections, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds the 2.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with stable federal funds rates indicate economic strengthening without immediate inflationary pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 100 basis points over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a housing market correction phase signal weakening construction sector earnings and broader credit contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of weeks during identified housing correction periods.
